prevalence.trend: stable; seasonal peaks in school periods

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; significant school absenteeism and antibiotic use

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment (Centor/McIsaac) with rapid antigen testing or culture when available to confirm strep

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High for symptom relief; antibiotic stewardship varies by diagnostic access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; confirmatory testing limited in some settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care for viral; penicillin/amoxicillin for suspected or confirmed streptococcal pharyngitis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antibiotic overuse without testing and delayed ENT access for recurrent cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost antibiotics and primary care access across SUS
